title: MAXAR_temporal_light_traits light extension
description: glTF extension to KHR_lights_punctual for specifying the temporal traits for individual lights

flashing.description: The flashing trait describes a simple flashing pattern that can be characterized by a waveform type and frequency. This trait modulates the light's intensity over time according to the specified parameters.

amplitudeOffset.description: Offset amplitude from default range of (0.0, 1.0). This value is added to the waveform output after scaling.

amplitudeScale.description: Scale amplitude from default range of (0.0, 1.0). The waveform output is multiplied by this value before adding the offset.

duty.description: Portion of period the light is on as normalized value [0.0, 1.0]. Only applicable for square waveforms. A value of 0.5 creates equal on/off periods, while 0.25 creates short pulses.

frequency.description: Frequency of the waveform in Hertz (Hz). Must be a positive value. Higher frequencies create faster flashing patterns.

phaseOffset.description: Phase offset in seconds. This shifts the waveform in time, allowing multiple lights to flash in synchronized or offset patterns.

waveform.description: Shape of the waveform that defines the flashing pattern.
